name: La Garganta del río Moros — circular de La Estación de El Espinar
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Espinar offers diverse landscapes, from river plains and extensive pine forests to the challenging terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The region is characterized by varied topography, including oak groves, poplar groves, and vast cereal plains, providing a range of cycling environments. Altitudes range from 1,050 meters to 2,197 meters, with prominent peaks such as Cabeza Líjar and Pico del Oso. Major rivers like the Moros, Gudillos, and Voltoya wind through the municipality, adding to its natural features. Best touring cycling routes around El Espinar The most popular touring cycling route is La Garganta del río Moros — circular de La Estación de El Espinar, a 29.0 miles (46.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses varied landscapes, including river gorges and forested areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Las Cabritillas Spring – Tejo Reservoir loop from San Rafael, a difficult 23.7 miles (38.2 km) path. This route offers scenic views, passing by the Tejo Reservoir and through natural surroundings. Local touring cyclists also love the El Espinar – El Espinar Town Hall loop from San Rafael, a 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail leading through local areas and varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around El Espinar is defined by diverse forests, river plains, and the mountain terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in El Espinar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 160 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Espinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in El Espinar?
A: El Espinar offers a variety of touring cycling experiences with nearly 20 routes documented on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in El Espinar?
A: You can expect highly diverse terrain. Routes traverse picturesque oak groves, poplar groves, and extensive pine forests along river plains, interspersed with vast cereal plains. The region also borders the Sierra de Guadarrama, offering challenging climbs and descents with panoramic views.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options for less experienced cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Espinar – El Espinar Town Hall loop from San Rafael&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.0 km (9.9 miles) trail that can be completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes, offering a pleasant ride through local areas.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the El Espinar area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190502995/la-garganta-del-rio-moros-circular-de-la-estacion-de-el-espinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Garganta del río Moros — circular de La Estación de El Espinar&lt;/a&gt; (46.7 km / 29.0 miles with 847m elevation gain) or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cabritillas Spring – Tejo Reservoir loop from San Rafael&lt;/a&gt; (38.2 km / 23.7 miles with 621m elevation gain) offer significant climbs and varied landscapes, including river gorges and mountain views.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around El Espinar?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in El Espinar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190502995/la-garganta-del-rio-moros-circular-de-la-estacion-de-el-espinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Garganta del río Moros — circular de La Estación de El Espinar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cabritillas Spring – Tejo Reservoir loop from San Rafael&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: El Espinar's routes offer access to significant natural features and historical landmarks. You can cycle through diverse forests, along river plains, and near the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1699249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Jarosa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Historical attractions like the ancient Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría are also accessible, blending history with natural beauty.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes or viewpoints worth visiting by bike?
A: Absolutely. The proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama means you can encounter several prominent mountain passes. Notable ones include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1771270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Malagón&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4220056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del León&lt;/a&gt;, all offering spectacular views of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in El Espinar?
A: The best time to cycle in El Espinar is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer mornings are also popular, especially in the mountains, to avoid the heaviest traffic and heat. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in El Espinar?
A: The touring cycling routes around El Espinar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil river plains to the challenging and rewarding ascents of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Is El Espinar a good destination for family touring cycling trips?
A: El Espinar offers a range of routes, including moderate ones that could be suitable for families, especially those with some cycling experience. The varied terrain and scenic beauty provide an engaging environment for family outings. It's advisable to choose shorter, less strenuous routes and check elevation profiles for suitability.

Q: How can I get to the touring cycling routes in El Espinar using public transport?
A: El Espinar is accessible by public transport, though specific routes may require a short ride or walk from the nearest station or bus stop. It's recommended to check local bus and train schedules to El Espinar or San Rafael, as these towns serve as common starting points for many routes in the area.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in El Espinar?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns of El Espinar and San Rafael, which are common starting points for many touring cycling routes. Look for designated parking areas within these towns. Always ensure you park responsibly and check for any local parking regulations.
tips: The largest of the headwater reservoirs of the Moros River. Wonderful place

A pretty massive church for the manageable place. The Parish Church of San Eutropio. Its oldest parts date from the 13th century. In 1572 it was rebuilt by a student of Juan de Herrera using stylistic elements from El Escorial. At the main portal is a statue of Saint Eutropius, said to have been brought by French immigrants from Saintes when they were resettled after the Reconquista.
And storks can be found everywhere here.
Pretty little town hall on the main square.
